senses.g: Tamil Nadu (a state in southern India)

تاناشاہ.e: Compound of طَعْنَہ (ta'na, “taunting”) + شاہ (śāh, “king, ruler”). Coincidentially, also coined after Abu'l Hasan 'Tana Shah' the genial benevolent ruler of the 17th century Deccan kingdom of Golconda, wrongly portrayed as a vile and perverted despot in contemporary chronicles.

تانبا.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀁𑀩 (taṃba), from Sanskrit ताम्र (tāmrá).

تاؤ.e: From the Wade-Giles romanization of Chinese 道 (dào, literally “The Way”).

تاہم.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تا هم (tā-ham, “still, yet, nevertheless”). By surface analysis, تا (tā) + ہم (ham).

تایا.e: Inherited from Middle Hindi تایا (taya /⁠tāyā⁠/) (c. 1435), from Sanskrit *तातिय (*tātiya) / तात्य (tātyá). Cognate with Punjabi تایْا (tāyā) / ਤਾਇਆ (tāiā).

تب.e: From Sanskrit त (ta, “this; that”) + एवम् एव (evam eva, “just so”).

ex.u: انٹربینک تبادلہ
ex.e: interbank exchange

تبادلہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تبادل (tabādul), from Arabic تَبَادُل (tabādul).

senses.g: Tibet (a geographic region in Central Asia, the homeland of the Tibetan people)

تبدیل.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تبدیل (tabdīl), from Arabic تَبْدِيل (tabdīl).

senses.g: blessed gift; sacred relic (touched or blessed by a saint)
a blessed relic or gift (see بَرْکَت (barkat, “blessing”))

تبرک.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَبَرُّک (tabarruk), borrowed from Arabic تَبَرُّك (tabarruk), from تَبَرَّكَ (tabarraka). First attested in 1682 as Middle Hindi تبرک (tbrk /⁠tabarruk⁠/).

senses.g: The intense feeling of anger and jealousy when you see someone you love being close to someone else. It's more than jealousy—it comes from a deep emotional connection and possessiveness.

ex.u: جب میں نے اُسے کسی اور کے ساتھ دیکھا، تبریر دل میں اُتر گئی۔
ex.e: When I saw her laughing with someone else, a wave of tabrīr hit me out of nowhere.

تبریر.e: Neo-coined Urdu word, possibly inspired by Arabic root, coined in modern times.

senses.g: Tabriz (a city in East Azerbaijan Province, Iran)

تبسم.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَبَسُّم (tabassum) from Arabic تَبَسُّم (tabassum).

تپ دق.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَپِ دِق (tap-i diq). By surface analysis, تپ + دق.

ex.u: یہ تپسوی جنگل میں رہتے ہیں۔
ex.r: ye tapasvī jaṅgal mẽ rahte ha͠i.
ex.e: These ascetics live in the forest.

ex.u:
لمحے اُڑتے ہیں کبھی یا تو تتلیوں کی طرح
یا کبھی خوشبوؤں کی مانند چیخ اُٹھتے ہیں
ex.e:
Moments fly away, sometimes like butterflies,
or sometimes they scream like fragrances.

تتلی.e: Inherited from Middle Hindi تیتری (tytry /⁠tītrī⁠/) / تتری (titrī), from Prakrit 𑀢𑀺𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀭𑀺 (tittiri), ultimately from Sanskrit तित्तिर (tittirá). Dasa, Qureshi relate it to تِیتَر (tītar, “partridge”). Compare Punjabi تِتْلِی (titlī) / ਤਿਤਲੀ (titlī), Bengali তিতলি (titli).

تجارت.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تِجَارَت (tijārat), from Arabic تِجَارَة (tijāra).

تجاوز.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَجَاوُز (tajāwuz), borrowed from Arabic تَجَاوُز (tajāwuz), from تَجَاوَزَ (tajāwaza).

تجربہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تجربه (tajriba, tajruba), from Arabic تَجْرِبَة (tajriba).

تجزیہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تجزیه (tajziya, “dividing; analysis”), from Arabic تَجْزِئة (tajziʔa, “division”).

تجسس.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَجَسُّس (tajassus), from Arabic تَجَسُّس (tajassus).

senses.g: second-person singular objective personal pronoun: (oblique case) you, thee; Early Modern English thee.
second-person singular objective personal pronoun, intimate or very polite: thee

تجھ.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुज्झु (tujjhu), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀚𑁆𑀛𑀁 (tujjhaṃ), from Sanskrit तुभ्यम् (tubhyam).

senses.g: you, thee, Early Modern English thee.

تجھ.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुज्झु (tujjhu), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀚𑁆𑀛𑀁 (tujjhaṃ), from Sanskrit तुभ्यम् (tubhyam).

تحریر.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تحریر (tahrīr) from Arabic تحرير (taḥrīr).

senses.g: movement (ie. an organisation with a specific objective)

تحریک.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تحریک (tahrīk), from Arabic تَحْرِيك (taḥrīk).

تحصیل.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَحْصِیل (tahsīl), from Arabic تَحْصِيل (taḥṣīl).

senses.g: learning by heart, committing to memory

تحفہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تحفه (tuhfa), from Arabic تُحْفَة (tuḥfa).

تحقیق.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تحقیق (tahqīq), from Arabic تَحْقِيق (taḥqīq).

تختہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تخته (taxta), from Middle Persian tʾht' (taxt, “throne”) + -k' (-ag).

تخم.e: From Classical Persian تخم (tuxm, “seed; semen; origin”).

تخمہ.e: From Classical Persian تخمه (tuxma, “descent; extraction, stock”).

تخیل.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَخَیُّل (taxayyul), from Arabic تَخَيُّل (taḵayyul).

senses.g: tarawih (an extra prayer performed by Sunni Muslims at night in the month of Ramadan)

تراویح.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَرَاوِیح (tarāwīh), from Arabic تَرَاوِيحْ (tarāwīḥ).

senses.g: foothills; plateau at the foot of a mountain

ترائی.e: Inherited from Prakrit *तलघट्टिका- (*talaghaṭṭikā-), ultimately derived from Proto-Indo-Aryan *taras (“plain, plateau”), possibly related to Sanskrit तल (tala, “level”); cognate to Nepali तराइ (tarāi). Possibly later associated with Classical Persian تر (tar, “humid”).

Inherited from Prakrit *तलघट्टिका- (*talaghaṭṭikā-), ultimately derived from Proto-Indo-Aryan *taras (“plain, plateau”), possibly related to Sanskrit तल (tala, “level”); cognate to Nepali तराइ (tarāi). Possibly later associated with Classical Persian تر (tar, “humid”).

senses.g: Turbat (a city in Kech district, Balochistan, Pakistan)

تربوز.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تربوز (tarbūz), variant of تربز (tarbuz).

senses.g: upbringing (ie. of a parent to a child)

تربیت.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَرْبِیَت (tarbiyat), borrowed from Arabic تَرْبِيَة (tarbiya), from رَبَّى (rabbā). First attested in c. 1695 as Middle Hindi تربیت (trbyt).

ترتیب.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَرْتِیب (tartīb), borrowed from Arabic تَرْتِيب (tartīb).

senses.g: truti (Hindu unit of time equivalent to 0.03-35 microseconds)

ترجمان.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian ترجمان (tarjumān), from Arabic تَرْجُمَان (tarjumān).

ترجمہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian ترجمه (tarjama), from Arabic تَرْجَمَة (tarjama).

ترجیح.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَرْجِیح (tarjīh), from Arabic تَرْجِيح (tarjīḥ).

ex.u: تجھ پہ ترس آتا ہے۔
ex.e: I feel sorry for you.

ترس.e: Inherited from Sanskrit [Term?], from the root तृष् (tṛṣ), ultimately derived from Proto-Indo-European *ters-.

senses.g: two days after tomorrow, in three days
two days before yesterday, three days ago

ترسوں.e: Inherited from Sanskrit अतिपरश्वस् (atiparaśvas, “beyond overmorrow”) or त्रिश्वस् (triśvas, “third morrow”).

ترقی.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَرَقِّی (taraqqī), borrowed from Arabic تَرَقٍّ (taraqqin), from تَرَقَّى (taraqqā).

senses.g: a Turkic person; a speaker of a Turkic language, or bearer of Turkic heritage
a Turkish person; a citizen of Turkey

ترک.e: From Classical Persian تُرک (turk), or conversely through Chagatai.

ترکاری.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تره (tara, “vegetable”) + Tamil கறி (kaṟi). Compare Assamese তৰকাৰী (torkari), Bengali তরকারী (torkari).

senses.g: of or belonging to a Turk; Turkish, Turk-like

ترکی.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian ترکی (turkī). By surface analysis, ترک (turk) + ـی (-ī).

senses.g: Turk-like qualities or habits, oppression, haughtiness, insolence, etc. (see ترک (turk))

ترکی.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian ترکی (turkī). By surface analysis, ترک (turk) + ـی (-ī).

senses.g: Turkey (a country in Asia and Europe) [from 20th c.], Republic of Turkey

ترکیہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تُرْکِیَّه (turkiyya), from Arabic تُرْكِيَّة (turkiyya). Displaced in the 20th century by تُرْکِی (turkī) used as a phono-semantic matching of English Turkey. Regaining usage in the 21st century under the influence of Turkish Türkiye, of same origin (see Usage notes).

تسبیح.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَسْبِیح (tasbīh), from Arabic تَسْبِيح (tasbīḥ).

senses.g: contentment, satisfaction, consolation, comfort, solace, assurance

تسلی.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَسَلّی (tasallē), from Arabic تَسَلٍّ (tasallin).

تسلیم.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تسلیم (taslīm), from Arabic تَسْلِيم (taslīm).

تسمہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تسمه, from Ancient Greek δεσμός (desmós).

تشدد.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَشَدُّد (tašaddud), borrowed from Arabic تَشَدُّد (tašaddud).

تشریح.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تشریح (tašrīh), from Arabic تَشْرِيح (tašrīḥ).

تشریف.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَشْرِیف (tašrīf), borrowed from Arabic تَشْرِيف (tašrīf), from شَرَّفَ (šarrafa). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi تشریف (tśryf /⁠taśrīf⁠/).

senses.g: someone who creates distraction, discomfort, nervousness, anxiousness, restlessness

تشویشناک.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تشویشناک (tašwīšnāk), from تشویش (tašwīš) + ـناک (-nāk), the first element from Arabic.

تصدیق.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَصْدِیق (tasdīq), from Arabic تَصْدِيق (taṣdīq).

تصریف.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَصْرِیف (tasrīf), from Arabic تَصْرِيف (taṣrīf).

تصمیم.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَصْمِیم (tasmīm), from Arabic verbal noun تَصْمِيم (taṣmīm).

تصویر.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَصْوِیر (taswīr), borrowed from Arabic تَصْوِير (taṣwīr), from صَوَّرَ (ṣawwara). First attested in c. 1500 as Middle Hindi تَصْوِیر (tṣvyr).

تعارف.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعَارُف (ta'āruf), borrowed from Arabic تَعَارُف (taʕāruf), from تَعَارَفَ (taʕārafa).

senses.g: explanation; putting a new construction (on words)

تعبیر.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعْبِیر (ta'bīr), borrowed from Arabic تَعْبِير (taʕbīr), from عَبَّر (ʕabbar). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi تعبیر (t'byr /⁠ta'bīr⁠/).

تعجب.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعَجُّب (ta'ajjub), borrowed from Arabic تَعَجُّب (taʕajjub). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi تعجب (t'jb).

تعداد.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تعداد (ta'dād), from Arabic تَعْدَاد (taʕdād).

senses.g: a numerous number; (the state of being more than one)
frequency (in all senses, like time, statistics and physics)

تعدد.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعَدُّد (ta'addud), from Arabic تَعَدُّد (taʕaddud).

senses.g: a table of rates of export and import duties

تعریف.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعْرِیف (ta'rīf), borrowed from Arabic تَعْرِيف (taʕrīf), from عَرَّفَ (ʕarrafa).

تعزیت.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعْزِیَت (ta'ziyat), from Arabic تَعْزِيَة (taʕziya).

senses.g: tazia (a representation of the tombs of Hasan and Husayn (the grandsons of Muhammad), carried in the South Asian and Indo-Caribbean Shiite processions of Muharram)

تعطیل.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعْطِیل (ta'tīl), from Arabic verbal noun تَعْطِيل (taʕṭīl, “suspension, break, hindrance”), from verb عَطَّلَ (ʕaṭṭala, “to suspend, to inactivate”).

تعلق.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعَلُّق (ta'alluq), borrowed from Arabic تَعَلُّق (taʕalluq), from تَعَلَّقَ (taʕallaqa). First attested in c. 1582 as Middle Hindi تعلق (t'lq /⁠ta'alluq⁠/).

senses.g: oblique plural connection; relationship of تَعَلُّق (ta'alluq)

ex.u: اس باب میں انیسویں صدی کی ان کتابوں کا ذکر کیا جائے گا جو ابتدائی تعلیم کے لئے ایرانی طرز سے ہٹ کر لکھی گئیں
ex.e: In this chapter, the books of the 19th century that were written contrasting to the Iranian style for primary education will be mentioned

ex.u: میں آپ کو یقین دلاتا ہوں کہ یہ میری تعلیم اس کو نہیں ہے۔
ex.e: Believe me, this is not my teaching [that I have given] to him.

ex.u: استاد جی ... ارباب نشاط کے تعلیم دینے والے کو کہتے ہیں، خود لفظ تعلیم بھی لکھنؤ کی زبان میں اس خاص معنے میں مستعمل ہے۔
ex.e: 'Ustaad ji' ... is one who gives training in arts [relating to singing and dancing], the word "taʻlīm" itself is used in this way in the Lucknow vernacular.

senses.g: teaching, knowledge, guidance (attained by or given to a disciple, student)

ex.u: اور مسیح نے حواری کو اپنی زبان مبارک سے تعلیم دی ہؠ
ex.e: And Christ has given the apostles guidance in his blessed language

تعلیم.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعْلِیم (ta'līm), borrowed from Arabic تَعْلِيم (taʕlīm), from عَلَّمَ (ʕallama).

تعویذ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعْوِیذ (ta'wīz), borrowed from Arabic تَعْوِيذ (taʕwīḏ).

senses.g: unmindfulness, heedlessness, forgetfulness, neglect, negligence, inattention, inadvertence, indifference, listlessness

تغییر.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تغییر (taġyīr), from Arabic تَغْيِير (taḡyīr).
Borrowed from Classical Persian تغییر (taġyīr), from Arabic تَغْيِير (taḡyīr).

تفصیل.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَفْصِیل (tafsīl), from Arabic تَفْصِيل (tafṣīl). First attested in c. 1645 as Middle Hindi تفصیل (tfṣyl).

تفنگ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تفنگ (tufang), perhaps from Khorezmian Turkic, but more likely via Chagatai through the Mughal Empire whose rulers were initially Chagatai-speaking, and established one of the three "gunpowder empires" alongside the Ottoman Empire and Safavid Iran. The word is itself derived as an onomatopoeia mimicking the sound of blowpipes used to kill birds.

تقدس.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقَدُّس (taqaddus), borrowed from Arabic تَقَدُّس (taqaddus).

تقدیر.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقْدِیر (taqdīr), from Arabic تَقْدِير (taqdīr).

senses.g: around, about, approximately, close to, almost, nearly

ex.u: میری جیب میں تَقْرِیباً ۵۰۰ رُوپَے ہَیں
ex.r: merī jeb mẽ taqrīban 500 rūpai ha͠i
ex.e: I have around 500 rupees in my pocket

تقریبا.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقْرِیباً (taqrīban), borrowed from Arabic تَقْرِيبًا (taqrīban).

تقسیم.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقْسِیم (taqsīm), borrowed from Arabic تَقْسِيم (taqsīm), from قَسَّمَ (qassama).

ex.u: تقطيع کرنا
ex.e: perform scansion

senses.g: caesura, in the context of poetry (especially Urdu poetry)

تقطیع.e: From Arabic تَقْطِيع (taqṭīʕ), verbal noun of قَطَعَ (qaṭaʕa).

تقلید.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تقلید (taqlīd), from Arabic تَقْلِيد (taqlīd).

senses.g: taqwa – fear of God; piety; abstinence

تقوی.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقْویٰ (taqwā), borrowed from Arabic تَقْوَى (taqwā).

تقویم.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقْوِیم (taqwīm), from Arabic تَقْوِيم (taqwīm).

تکرار.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَکْرَار (takrār), from Arabic تَكْرار (takrār).

تکلیف.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَکْلِیف (taklīf), borrowed from Arabic تَكْلِيف (taklīf), from كَلَّفَ (kallafa).

senses.g: anything upon which one leans, prop, support
takya (all senses) — the stand or abode of a faqīr

تکھان.e: PIE word *tétḱō Inherited from Sanskrit तक्षन् (takṣan, “carpenter; woodcutter”), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *táṭṣā, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *táćšā, from Proto-Indo-European *tétḱō (“carpenter, woodcutter”).

تگڑا.e: Inherited from Prakrit *𑀢𑀕𑁆𑀕 (*tagga) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀟- (-ḍa-) + -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit *तर्ग (*targa, “frightening; terrible; strong”), from the root तर्ज् (tarj, “to threaten; scold; frighten”).

تل.e: Inherited from Prakrit तिल (tila), from Sanskrit तिल (tila), borrowed from a Munda language.

senses.g: Tel Aviv (a city in central Israel)
Tel Aviv (a district in central Israel)

تل ابیب.e: Borrowed from Arabic تَلّ أَبِيب (tall ʔabīb), ultimately from Hebrew תֵּל אָבִיב (tel āvīv).

ex.u: گھر واپس آئے تو ہر جگہ زمین پر تل چٹوں کی لاشیں پڑی ہوئی تھیں۔
ex.r: ghar vāpas āye to har jagah zamīn par til caṭṭõ kī lāśẽ paṛĩ huī thī̃.
ex.e: When we got back home, there were cockroach corpses lying on the floor everywhere.

تل چٹا.e: From تیل (tel, “oil”) + چَاٹْنَا (cāṭnā, “to lick”), named so for its attraction to foodstuffs such as oil. Influenced by Sanskrit तैलपायिका (tailapāyikā). Compare Bengali তেলাপোকা (telapōka).

تلاوت.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تلاوت (tilāvat), taken from Arabic تِلَاوَة (tilāwa).

تلخی.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تلخی (talxī). Equivalent to تلخ (talx) + ـی (-ī).

تلفظ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَلَفُّظ (talaffuz), from Arabic تَلَفُّظ (talaffuẓ).

senses.g: Telangana (a state in south-central India, created in 2014 by separation from Andhra Pradesh)

تلوار.e: Inherited from Middle Hindi تلْوار (tlvar /⁠talvār⁠/) (c. 1606) / تروار (trvar /⁠tarvār⁠/) (c. 1611), from Sanskrit तरवारि (taravāri, “sword; scimitar”). Cognates Assamese তৰোৱাল (torüal), Bengali তলোয়ার (tolōẇar), Gujarati તલવાર (talvār), Kashmiri तरवार (tarawār), Konkani तलवार (talvār), Marathi तलवार (talvār), Marwari तरवार (tarvār), Nepali तरवार (tarawār), Odia ତରୁଆରି (taruāri), Punjabi تَلْوار / ਤਲਵਾਰ (talvār), and Sindhi ترارِ (trāri) / तरारि (tarāri).

senses.g: Talagang (a city in Talagang district, Punjab, Pakistan)

تلہ گنگ.e: Borrowed from Punjabi تلہ گنگ (tala gaṅg) / ਤਲਾਗੰਗ (talāgaṅg), named after the Hindus known as "Gangs" and due to it being below ground level, it was known as "tala".

senses.g: any crop used to make oil
second-person subjective or objective personal pronoun, grammatically plural: you, ye; Early Modern English ye.

ex.u: کیا تم مجھ سے محبت کرتی ہو؟
ex.r: kyā tum mujh-se mohabbat kartī ho?
ex.e: Do you love me?

تم.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुम्हि (tumhi), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (tumhe), from Sanskrit युष्मान् (yuṣmān, acc. pl.) adopting the initial sounds of त्वम् (tvám, nom. sg.).

senses.g: The individual or group spoken or written to; you, ye, Early Modern English ye.

ex.u: تم احمق کوئی کام کے نہیں ہو۔
ex.r: tum ahmaq ko'ī kām ke nahī̃ ho.
ex.e: You idiots are of no use.

تم.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुम्हि (tumhi), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (tumhe), from Sanskrit युष्मान् (yuṣmān, acc. pl.) adopting the initial sounds of त्वम् (tvám, nom. sg.).

تماشا.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تماشا (tamāšā), ultimately from Arabic مَشَى (mašā, “to walk”). Compare Bengali তামাশা (tamaśa).

تماشہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تماشا (tamāšā), ultimately from Arabic مَشَى (mašā, “to walk”). Compare Bengali তামাশা (tamaśa).

تمام.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تمام (tamām), from Arabic تَمَام (tamām), from the root ت م م (t m m).

تمبوڑی.e: Borrowed from Punjabi ਧੰਬੋੜੀ (dhamboṛī) / دَھنْبوڑِی (dhanboṛī, “yellow paper wasp”).

senses.g: Tump (a city in Kech district, Balochistan, Pakistan)
tax on travellers, a stamp-tax for imported goods, toll tax

تمغا.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تمغا (tamġā), from Chagatai تمغا (tamğā).

تمنا.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَمَنَّا (tamannā), from Arabic تَمَنَّى (tamannā).

senses.g: a female given name from Arabic

تمنا.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَمَنَّا (tamannā), from Arabic تَمَنَّى (tamannā).

senses.g: vocative plural of تَمَنّا (tamannā, “wish; desire, longing, aspiration, supplication”)
oblique plural of تَمَنّا (tamannā, “wish; desire, longing, aspiration, supplication”)
direct plural of تَمَنّا (tamannā, “wish; desire, longing, aspiration, supplication”)

تمنچہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تمنچه (tamanča), from Ottoman Turkish طبانجه (tabanca, “pistol”).

senses.g: second-person possessive pronoun, grammatically plural: yours; Early Modern English yours.

تمہارا.e: Probably from Apabhramsa तुम्हारा (tumhārā), formed from Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (tumhe) or Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂𑀳𑀺𑀁 (tumhehiṃ) + را (rā, genitive affix) by analogy with 𑀅𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (amhe) or 𑀅𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂𑀳𑀺𑀁 (amhehiṃ) to अम्हारा (amhārā) or possibly inherited from Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑀸𑀡𑀁 (tumhāṇaṃ), genitive of 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (tumhe).

senses.g: second-person possessive determiner, grammatically plural: your; Early Modern English your.

تمہارا.e: Probably from Apabhramsa तुम्हारा (tumhārā), formed from Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (tumhe) or Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂𑀳𑀺𑀁 (tumhehiṃ) + را (rā, genitive affix) by analogy with 𑀅𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (amhe) or 𑀅𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂𑀳𑀺𑀁 (amhehiṃ) to अम्हारा (amhārā) or possibly inherited from Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑀸𑀡𑀁 (tumhāṇaṃ), genitive of 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (tumhe).

senses.g: manners, etiquette, decorum, politeness, respect, sense

تمیز.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تمیز (tamīz), itself from Arabic تَمْيِيز (tamyīz).

تن.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀡𑀽 (taṇū), from Sanskrit तनू (tanū́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *tanúHs, from Proto-Indo-European *tenúh₂s, from *tenuh₂- (“body”).

تناول.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَنَاوُل (tanāwul), from Arabic تَنَاوُل (tanāwul).

تنبولی.e: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀢𑀁𑀩𑁄𑀮𑀺𑀅 (taṃbolia), from Sanskrit ताम्बूलिक (tāmbūlika).

تندرست.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تندرست (tandurust). By surface analysis, تَن (tan, “body”) + دُرُسْت (durust, “good, healthy”).

تنظیم.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تنظیم (tanzīm), from Arabic تَنْظِيم (tanẓīm).

تنقید.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تنقید (tanqīd), a pseudo-Arabism.

تنہا.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تنها (tanhā), from Middle Persian tn'yhʾ (tanīhā).
Borrowed from Classical Persian تنها (tanhā), from Middle Persian tn'yhʾ (tanīhā).

ex.u: میں بچ بھی جاؤں تو تنہائی مار ڈالے گی
ex.r: ma͠i bac bhī jāõ to tanhāī mār ḍālegī
ex.e: Even if I survive, loneliness will kill me

تنہائی.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تنهایی (tanhāyī). By surface analysis, تنہا (tanhā) + ـی (-ī).

senses.g: second-person singular subjective personal pronoun: you, thou; Early Modern English thou.
second-person singular subjective personal pronoun, intimate or very polite: Thou

ex.u: اے خدا تو ہی میرا مددگار ہے!
ex.r: ai xudā tū-hī merā madadgār hai!
ex.e: O God, only Thou art my helper!

تو.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुहुं (tuhũ), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑀁 (tumaṃ), 𑀢𑀼𑀁 (tuṃ), from Sanskrit त्वम् (tvam).

senses.g: the individual spoken or written to; you, thou, Early Modern English thou.

تو.e: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa तुहुं (tuhũ), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀼𑀫𑀁 (tumaṃ), 𑀢𑀼𑀁 (tuṃ), from Sanskrit त्वम् (tvam).
Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit, probably from Sanskrit ततस् (tatas, “from there”).

ex.u: اِسے تو نے کھایا؟
ex.e: Did you eat this?

توا.e: From Sanskrit तापक (tāpaka). Cognate with Persian تابه (tâbe) and تاوه (tâve).

ex.u: ہَاں سَجْدے میں ہَم نے مان٘گا تھا، عُمْر بھی ہَماری لَگ جائے تُم کو، خود سی ہی تَوبَہ کَرْتے تھے، نَظَر نَہ ہَماری لَگ جائے تُم کو
ex.r: hā̃ sajde mẽ ham ne māṅgā thā, 'umr bhī hamārī lag jāe tum ko, xud sī hī tauba karte the, nazar na hamārī lag jāe tum ko

توبہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَوْبَه (tawba), from Arabic تَوْبَة (tawba).
Borrowed from Classical Persian تَوْبَه (tawba), from Arabic تَوْبَة (tawba).

توتا.e: Possibly from Classical Persian توته (tōta, “parrot”), طوطی (tūtī). Compare Punjabi ਤੋਤਾ (totā) / طوطا (t̤ot̤ā), Sindhi طوطو (t̤ot̤o) / तोतो (toto).

توجہ.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَوَجُّه (tawajju), from Arabic تَوَجُّه (tawajjuh). First attested in c. 1657 as Middle Hindi توجہ (tvjh /⁠tavajjuh⁠/).

تودا ربا.e: From Hebrew תּוֹדָה רַבָּה (todá rabá, “thank you very much”).

ex.u: ہم تودہ پر چڑھ گئے
ex.e: we climbed up the mound

ex.u: یہ ہمارے گاؤں کا ٹوڈا ہے
ex.r: ye hamare ga'õ kā tūda he
ex.e: this is the boundary of our village

ex.u: تودہ گلیوں میں جمع ہو گیا
ex.r: tūda galīyõ mẽ jama' ho gyā
ex.e: a crowd has gathered in the streets

senses.g: an earthen mound used to fire ranged weapons behind

ex.u: ساتھی تودہ پر تفنگ چاؤ
ex.e: comrade, fire the gun from the mound!

تودہ.e: From Classical Persian توده (tōda, “heap, pile; mass”).

تورات.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَوْرَات (tawrāt), from Arabic تَوْرَاة (tawrāh).

senses.g: Turan (a region of Central Asia, originally populated by Iranian Central Asian nomads; later, Iranians came to identify Turkic neighbours as Turanians)
Turkestan; the realm of the Turkic peoples

توقع.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian توقع (tawaqqu'), from Arabic تَوَقُّع (tawaqquʕ).

تولد.e: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَوَلُّد (tawallud), from Arabic تَوَلُّد (tawallud).
Borrowed from Classical Persian تَوَلُّد (tawallud), from Arabic تَوَلُّد (tawallud).
